title: The Border
openLocation: Southeastern Eversong Woods, on Farstrider patrol near the Amani frontier.
transport: Thalassian horses for Sylvanas, Lor'themar, and the mounted patrol.
immediateObjective: Finish the troll sweep, dodge the Convocation budget session, and stay outdoors one more day.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas perception only.
No trust or partnership framing between Sylvanas and Jaina yet.
No on-page Jaina dialogue before the chapter-boundary line into Chapter 2.

povKnowsAtOpen: Routine Amani troll pressure on the southeastern frontier.
The ward network is stable as far as the formal reports say.
The Farstriders maintain dossiers on powerful human mages near the border.
Jaina Proudmoore appears in those reports as a human mage worth watching.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: That a human mage has crossed the ward boundary.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No trust between Sylvanas and Jaina.
No proof yet that this is anything more than a human trespass.
No dialogue from Jaina before the final line.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Jaina is in Farstrider custody and must make her case directly to Sylvanas.
Sylvanas knows the prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore, not a random trespasser.
The border breach has turned from patrol annoyance into a serious interrogation.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: Anything south of the wards stays somebody else's trouble until it touches Sylvanas's stones, roads, or riders.
pov.surfaceObjective: Finish the troll work, stay on horseback, and find one more excuse to miss the Convocation.
pov.pressureSource: Cyndia's runner finds her with word that a human mage crossed the wards and will not stop talking about something wrong in the leylines.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas hears human mage and thinks politics, spying, paperwork. If the trouble started in Lordaeron, she expects Lordaeron to keep it there.
pov.chapterTest: The first warning that matters comes from the exact kind of outsider she is built to distrust, and it lands inside her own border instead of safely beyond it.
pov.forcedChoice: Leave the prisoner to protocol and let other people sort the mess out, or ride south and put her own eyes on it.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close this is no longer a spoiled patrol day. It is Sylvanas's problem, and the mage is standing in her holding space.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 2 opens with Sylvanas angry, alert.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The captured human mage who ruins an easy day before she even opens her mouth.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: A Kirin Tor intrusion, a spy, or some polished court sorceress who expects the border to bend for her.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Before Jaina even speaks, the worn boots, ink stains, frost-bitten knuckles, and raw-edged posture say fieldwork, exhaustion, and frustration instead of court polish.
throughPov.interactionRule: Inside Sylvanas's head, Jaina stays the mage, the human, or the prisoner. No warmth. No name-softening.
throughPov.doNotRevealYet: The full contamination case, her isolation from the Kirin Tor, or her desperation.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: No partnership. One woman in custody, one woman deciding how hard the door stays shut.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: Jaina stops being paperwork trouble and becomes the one person Sylvanas has to hear before she can decide what kind of threat this is.
team.namingRule: Sylvanas does not give Jaina the dignity of her name in her internal framing yet.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No thaw, no rapport, no early respect. The chapter ends on the first hard face-to-face look.

entries.rule: Present in Scenes 1-2. Exits north in Scene 2 to brief the Convocation.
Present in Scenes 1-2. Leaves north with Lor'themar once the report lands.
Enters in Scene 3 as border-post officer. Remains active into Chapter 2.
Scene 2 only. Delivers the inciting report, then turns back south with Sylvanas to Cyndia's post.
Seen in Scene 3. She does not speak on-page until the chapter-ending line prompts Chapter 2.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 2 opens at Cyndia's post on the interrogation beat. Lor'themar and the mounted patrol are already northbound; Sylvanas, Jaina, and Cyndia inherit the scene.

scenes.title: The Hunt
scenes.sceneFunction: Show Sylvanas where she is happiest, and the easy rhythm she only has with Lor'themar.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Ordinary frontier work; nothing stranger than trolls.

enrichment.detail: Eversong should read as a curated amber-canopied homeland rather than generic forest, and the Amani pressure as routine frontier violence that the Farstriders have handled for generations.

beats.goal: Finish the sweep by killing the last troll raider.
beats.conflict: The final Amani axethrower breaks from cover with an attack already committed.
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas is at ease only on a live patrol.
She sees footing, range, and openings before anyone else does.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Any larger threat than routine troll violence.
sensoryAnchors: wet impact of the arrow through troll flesh
beats.transitionIntent: release-pressure: the clean kill opens room for banter and reminds us how badly Sylvanas wants to stay out here, away from politics.

beats.dilemma: Ride back for the Convocation budget session or steal one more stretch of road before duty catches up.
beats.decision: Stretch the patrol south under the excuse of checking the ward-stones.
newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar knows exactly when Sylvanas is lying to herself.
The Convocation is a standing pressure on Sylvanas's time and temperament.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the ward-stones actually need checking.
beats.transitionIntent: linger-ease: let the road stay easy for another minute.

beats.conflict: Lor'themar keeps pushing the political reality Sylvanas wants to ignore.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the ride she wants, but the Convocation remains waiting at the edge of the day.

newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar is the one who absorbs court irritation so Sylvanas can stay where she likes best.
This is Sylvanas's emotional baseline before the story disrupts it.
Cyndia would know exactly which southbound road to search if Sylvanas stole one more patrol before a Convocation day.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Any immediate threat south of them.
sensoryAnchors: amber canopy light over the track
shared pace on the forest road
beats.transitionIntent: intrusion-pressure: ease is broken by an urgent mounted approach from the south.

scenes.title: The Messenger
scenes.sceneFunction: Bring in the bad report and split Sylvanas from Lor'themar.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Still just a border day, as far as anyone knows.
scenes.arcPressure: The problem Sylvanas wanted to leave south of the wards has already reached her road.

enrichment.detail: Thalassian Pass is the southern mountain chokepoint where Quel'Thalas's ambient hum thins, the air cools, and the ward-stones are felt more than heard; border riding here should stay quiet, trained, and defense-grid familiar.

beats.conflict: A Farstrider runner comes in hard from Cyndia's post, sent north on the south patrol road because Cyndia knew exactly where Sylvanas would be if she stole one more patrol before the Convocation, and he brings bad news: a human mage crossed the first ward ring before dawn and was caught studying the ward-stones.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas loses the rest of her ordinary day, and the trouble immediately smells like Kirin Tor business and politics.

newOnPageInformation: There is a detained human mage inside the ward network.
She has no presented credentials.
She is claiming something is wrong in the leylines.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether she acts for the Kirin Tor, for herself, or for someone else.
sensoryAnchors: runner speaking in clipped patrol shorthand
beats.transitionIntent: decision-pressure: someone has to handle the politics, and someone has to ride south.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Let Lor'themar make one dry remark that this was not blind luck: Cyndia sent the runner up the south patrol road because she knew Sylvanas would still be out here dodging the Convocation.

beats.dilemma: Someone must contain the political fallout while someone with real authority evaluates the prisoner.
beats.decision: Lor'themar rides north to the Convocation; Sylvanas rides south to the Pass.
newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar goes where councils talk; Sylvanas goes where trouble is still alive.
Both understand the stakes of an unauthorized human mage inside the ward system.
Lor'themar takes the patrol north, while the runner wheels back south with Sylvanas.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this is a diplomatic mess, a spy problem, or something worse.
sensoryAnchors: two horses separating at the fork
forest road north versus the harder southern track
the riderless gap after Lor'themar peels away
beats.transitionIntent: forward-drive: Sylvanas rides south without the one person who usually takes the edge off.

beats.goal: Reach the border post fast enough to control the situation personally.
beats.conflict: On the ride down, Sylvanas turns the possibilities over the way a soldier does: rogue mage, Kirin Tor spying, someone tampering with the wards.

outcome.summary: She arrives angrier and more certain it matters, but the talk of wrong leylines still does not fit any box she trusts.

newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas knows Jaina's name before she sees her face.
The border transition is both physical and mental for Sylvanas.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the mage is in over her head or deliberately hostile.
sensoryAnchors: Eversong warmth thinning into the colder Pass
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-pressure: the road carries her straight into Cyndia's account.

scenes.title: The Prisoner
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn a border arrest into a real problem and end on the hook.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Human mage detained; no identity confirmation yet; leyline claim still untrusted.
scenes.arcPressure: Seeing Jaina up close keeps this from staying a report on paper. Now it is a woman, a name, and a problem inside Sylvanas's own border post.

enrichment.detail: Jaina's Antonidas connection makes her a live sovereign-security problem, not a random trespasser, because she is already an elite Kirin Tor apprentice; Sylvanas's Ranger-General authority makes the name reveal immediately political as well as tactical.
enrichment.source: stories/the-plague-road/characters/jaina.md; stories/the-plague-road/characters/sylvanas.md; stories/the-plague-road/plans/story-arc.md; Warcraft Wiki: Jaina Proudmoore; Warcraft Wiki: Ranger-General of Silvermoon

beats.goal: Establish exactly what happened at the border.
beats.conflict: Cyndia confirms the mage crossed before dawn, reached the second ring, and argued for continued analysis instead of attempting escape.

outcome.summary: Cyndia gives Sylvanas clean facts, but the facts make the prisoner stranger, not clearer.

newOnPageInformation: The mage was not sneaking carefully; she was intent on her work.
The ward-stones reacted like something had gone wrong in them, not like someone blasted her way through.
Cyndia has confiscated staff and materials pending senior authority.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the mage is arrogant, panicked, or both.
Whether the leyline warning is genuine.
sensoryAnchors: rangers holding the posture of extended alert
beats.transitionIntent: proximity-pressure: the report sends Sylvanas straight to the holding space.

beats.goal: Assess the prisoner and confirm identity.
beats.conflict: What Sylvanas expected does not match what she sees: practical travel gear, worn boots, calloused hands, frost-burned knuckles, and the posture of someone furious at not being heard.

outcome.summary: The woman in the holding space is not what Sylvanas expected, and at the same moment Cyndia names her: Jaina Proudmoore, Antonidas's apprentice, inside the ward grid.

newOnPageInformation: The prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore.
She does not read as a pampered court mage.
This is no longer a minor trespass case.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Why Jaina crossed the border alone.
Whether Antonidas knows where she is.
Whether the contamination claim is real.
sensoryAnchors: ink stains and frost burns on her hands
confiscated staff against the weapons rack
beats.transitionIntent: escalation-hook: identity pushes the scene from detention to interrogation.

beats.dilemma: Releasing Jaina is impossible, but holding her without hearing her out feels just as dangerous.
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas now treats Jaina as a real security problem.
The conversation itself becomes the chapter hook.
sensoryAnchors: rangers clearing a path without being told
blue ward-light climbing Sylvanas's boots as she steps closer
absolute stillness before the first exchange
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-boundary: Chapter 1 ends on Sylvanas's opening line, and Chapter 2 begins from Jaina's POV at that same moment.

departuresAlreadyLocked: Lor'themar is northbound toward Silvermoon and will not appear physically in Chapter 2.
The mounted patrol goes north with Lor'themar and is gone before Sylvanas reaches the outpost.
The runner returns to border-post duty after bringing Sylvanas in and does not remain active in Chapter 2.
factsLockedForNextChapter: Jaina has been detained for hours before Sylvanas arrives.
Sylvanas knows Jaina's identity before the conversation starts.
Jaina has not yet explained the contamination on-page.
The line "Start talking" belongs to the chapter boundary and must not be replayed from outside Jaina's POV in Chapter 2.
factsNotYetEarned: Any assumption that the danger has already become something everyone can see and name.

warnings: Beats 1-3 all shift upward in mood before the report lands, so the opening ease runs positive three beats in a row. The break at beat 4 is doing a lot of corrective work.
